2603.17831.title: RPMS: Enhancing LLM-Based Embodied Planning through Rule-Augmented Memory Synergy
2603.17831.paper_summary: LLM agents often fail in closed-world embodied environments because actions must satisfy strict preconditions -- such as location, inventory, and container states -- and failure feedback is sparse. We identify two structurally coupled failure modes: (P1) invalid action generation and (P2) state drift, each amplifying the other in a degenerative cycle. We present RPMS, a conflict-managed architecture that enforces action feasibility via structured rule retrieval, gates memory applicability via a lightweight belief state, and resolves conflicts between the two sources via rules-first arbitration. On ALFWorld (134 unseen tasks), RPMS achieves 59.7% single-trial success with Llama 3.1 8B (+23.9 pp over baseline) and 98.5% with Claude Sonnet 4.5 (+11.9 pp); of the 8B gain, rule retrieval alone contributes +14.9 pp (statistically significant), making it the dominant factor. A key finding is that episodic memory is conditionally useful: it harms performance on some task types when used without grounding, but becomes a stable net positive once filtered by current state and constrained by explicit action rules. Adapting RPMS to ScienceWorld with GPT-4 yields consistent gains across all ablation conditions (avg. score 54.0 vs. 44.9 for the ReAct baseline), providing transfer evidence that the core mechanisms hold across structurally distinct environments.
2603.17831.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理在封閉世界的具身環境中經常失敗，因為行動必須滿足嚴格的前置條件——例如位置、庫存和容器狀態——而失敗反饋稀疏。我們識別出兩種結構上耦合的失敗模式：（P1）無效行動生成和（P2）狀態漂移，這兩者在退化循環中相互增強。我們提出了 RPMS，一種衝突管理架構，通過結構化規則檢索強制行動可行性，通過輕量級信念狀態限制記憶適用性，並通過規則優先仲裁解決兩個來源之間的衝突。在 ALFWorld（134 個未見任務）上，RPMS 在 Llama 3.1 8B 下達到 59.7% 的單次試驗成功率（比基準增加 23.9 個百分點），在 Claude Sonnet 4.5 下達到 98.5%（比基準增加 11.9 個百分點）；在 8B 增益中，僅規則檢索貢獻了 14.9 個百分點（統計顯著），使其成為主導因素。一個關鍵發現是，情節記憶在有條件的情況下是有用的：在未經基礎的情況下使用時，對某些任務類型的性能有害，但一旦經過當前狀態過濾並受到明確行動規則的約束，就成為穩定的淨正面影響。將 RPMS 適應於 ScienceWorld 與 GPT-4 的結合在所有消融條件下均產生了一致的增益（平均得分 54.0 對比 ReAct 基準的 44.9），提供了核心機制在結構上不同的環境中保持有效的轉移證據。
2603.17831.author: Zhenhang Yuan et.al.
2603.17831.authors: Zhenhang Yuan, Shenghai Yuan, Lihua Xie
